text: As we open up the chest, there is no lung parenchyma that we can see and that is because of the thick slough and thick fibrous tissue that has completely entrapped the lung.
We can see them pointing out at the diaphragm and thick peel over the lung and strong lesions that they have.
So here we are decorting, we are removing the thick peel that has entrapped the lung from the visceral pleura while making sure no parenchymal injury is carried out.
Thick fibrous peel that have already been removed, you can see in the kidney tray above.
So, we saw two injuries secondary to the stab that were present, one of them being the lung parenchyma that was lacerated and this is being repaired in a continuous manner in single layer.
Additionally, a parenchymal injury that occurred epically during decortication is being repaired here.
Stab had caused a rent over the diaphragm although it had fibrous tissue over it but here we are repairing it so that it has better strength.
On completion of decod the lung is coming up to the chest wall and incidental finding of absent horizontal fissure therefore the upper and the middle lobe represent as a single lobe.
Chest tube is being placed here.
